sanskrit:
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलमसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा।
ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः॥
transliteration: aśvattham enaṃ suvirūḍha-mūlam asaṅga-śastreṇa dṛḍhena chittvā | tataḥ padaṃ tat parimārgitavyaṃ yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: This banyan tree, with roots well-grown, must be cut with the strong weapon of non-attachment; then one must seek that place from which, once gone, one returns no more.
contextual_meaning: The mind’s map of reality is an inverted, self-referential network whose origin, end, and ground are unknowable to ordinary cognition. Only by deliberately dis-identifying with every branch (role, memory, desire) using the “weapon” of mindful detachment can the psyche glimpse the dimension beyond the map—an irreversible shift into trans-personal being.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance resolved by decisive metacognitive severing of identification with the narrative self.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m., realizing the feed never ends and never satisfies, then choosing to delete all accounts and take a silent retreat.
anchor_text: Cut every clinging root to step beyond the mind’s mirrored maze.
